artifacts.summary: Firecrawl discovery and official HTTP checks located Uppsala University-controlled sources for central generative AI guidelines in teaching and examination, student guidance, student Copilot access, staff examination guidance, research support guidance, the local AI-guidelines index, and Faculty of Law assessment restrictions. Approved claims are limited to the exact central, student, staff, research, local-index, and faculty-scoped evidence. A generic staff AI landing page and a research blog post were rejected as too low-specificity or research-showcase-only for policy claims.

stopConditions: Stop after central, student-facing, staff, research, local-index, and explicitly faculty-scoped official evidence supports narrow claim candidates.

Do not mark staged candidates canonical or human-reviewed.

artifacts.claimText: Uppsala University's central generative AI guidelines say that any restriction on generative AI use in teaching or assessment, and any requirement to report AI use, must be clearly stated in written information; restrictions must be justifiable by learning outcomes, the nature of the task, or pedagogical considerations.

artifacts.claimText: When students are expected to use generative AI in teaching or assessment, Uppsala University's central guidelines say the course coordinator or examiner must be able to ensure the tools are provided at no cost to the student; if doctoral students are expected to use generative AI, the principal supervisor has responsibility for ensuring suitable tools are available at no cost.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Riktlinjer för användning av generativ AI i undervisning och examination
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Om användningen av generativ AI är begränsad vid undervisning och/eller examination, ska detta tydligt framgå av skriftlig information. Begränsningen ska kunna motiveras utifrån kunskapsmål, uppgiftens karaktär eller pedagogiska överväganden. Vid undervisning och examination ska det även tydligt framgå av skriftlig information om användning av generativ AI ska redovisas.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Restrictions or reporting requirements for generative AI in teaching or assessment must be clear in written information and restrictions must be justified by learning outcomes, task character, or pedagogy.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI tools for students
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students at Uppsala University have free access to the AI tool Copilot. When you log in with your student account, using Copilot is as safe as using other Microsoft online services (M365). When you log in, your interactions with Copilot are safeguarded through the university’s agreement for data protection with Microsoft ... chat data is not saved ... and no data is used to train the LLMs.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students have free Copilot access; when logged in with a student account, Copilot interactions are covered by Uppsala's Microsoft data-protection agreement and are not used to train LLMs.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI and studies
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If you have any questions about the use of generative AI in your course, please contact your teacher. Sometimes it is difficult to know where to draw the line between permissible and prohibited use of generative AI. If there is suspicion of prohibited use of genAI, this can be reported to the Disciplinary Board at Uppsala University, which may lead to sanctions such as suspension.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Student guidance directs course-specific AI questions to the teacher and says suspected prohibited use can be reported to the disciplinary board and may lead to sanctions.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using AI in Examinations
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Use UU’s Copilot if you upload students’ texts - not just any tools. And remove all identifying information from the text before uploading it. ... The EU AI Act considers examination to be a critical area where extra high demands are placed on users of generative AI, and for now, it is wise to refrain from letting AI grade exams.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Staff guidance advises using UU's Copilot and removing identifiers for uploaded student texts, and says it is wise for now to refrain from AI grading exams.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Uncertain ground 1 and Uncertain Ground 2 sections

artifacts.sourceTitle: Introduktion för användning av AI i forskningen
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Den här introduktionsguiden är ett praktiskt stöd för användandet av AI i forskningen vid Uppsala universitet. Den är inte en regelbok eller riktlinje, utan ska ses som ett enkelt verktyg för att komma i gång ... Snabbstart – checklista för att komma igång: Använd Copilot Chat via UU-inloggning; Dela aldrig känslig eller sekretessbelagd information i ett AI-verktyg; Dubbelkolla alltid AI-genererat material; Dokumentera hur du använder AI-verktygen.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The research AI introduction is practical support, not a rulebook; its quickstart says to use Copilot Chat via UU login, never share sensitive/confidential information, double-check AI output, and document AI use.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Lokala riktlinjer för AI
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Fler och fler institutioner, program, enheter och avdelningar vid Uppsala universitet tar fram egna riktlinjer för AI-användning. På denna sida samlar vi de lokala riktlinjerna för AI. ... Universitetet har fått sin första universitetsövergripande riktlinje för AI i undervisningen. Riktlinjen gäller från den 20 januari 2025. De nya riktlinjerna omfattar utbildning på alla nivåer från grundnivå till och med forskarutbildning.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Uppsala maintains a local AI-guidelines index and states that the university-wide teaching AI guideline applies from 20 January 2025 and covers all education levels through doctoral education.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Faculty of Law guidelines on restrictions on Generative AI (GAI) in assessment
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: These guidelines apply to all courses offered by the Faculty of Law and concern the use of generative AI tools (GAI) in assessment. ... The use of AI-generated material (e.g., text, images, or data) is not permitted in assessment components. ... GAI tools may only be used in assessment if this is explicitly stated in the course documentation for the relevant course. AI-based tools may be used for proofreading, but not for generating new content. The use of such tools for proofreading does not need to be reported.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Faculty of Law assessment guidance prohibits AI-generated material in assessment components unless course documentation explicitly permits it; proofreading tools may be used without reporting.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Scope, Main rule, and Exceptions sections
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the Swedish central guidelines directly use 'ska' for written restriction and reporting information and require restrictions to be justifiable.
Approved because the central Swedish guideline directly assigns students responsibility for central and local AI guideline awareness.
Approved because the central Swedish guideline directly says required AI tools must be available without cost to students and doctoral students.
Approved because the central Swedish guideline directly ties use of AI-generated material to user responsibility, academic probity, critical review, and ethics.
Approved because the central Swedish guideline directly states the approved-system limit for sensitive personal data and the student-permission limit for student material.
Approved as a student tool-access and data-protection claim because the official student AI-tool page directly states free Copilot access and Microsoft agreement protections.

Approved with student-facing wording because the official student page says students with course-specific AI questions should contact their teacher and prohibited use may be reported to the disciplinary board.
Approved with advisory wording because the staff guidance says to use UU's Copilot for uploaded student texts and says it is wise to refrain from letting AI grade exams.
Approved as practical research guidance only; the claim explicitly preserves that the page says it is not a rulebook or formal guideline.
Approved as a source-status claim because the official local-guidelines index directly states the existence of local AI guidelines and the scope/effective date of the university-wide guideline.
Approved because the Faculty of Law source directly states the restriction; the claim is explicitly scoped to Faculty of Law courses and assessment components.

artifacts.title: Uppsala University AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Uppsala University is based on public official Uppsala University-controlled sources. The Swedish central guidelines PDF supports claims on written restriction/reporting requirements, student responsibility for central and local guidelines, free access to required AI tools, responsibility for AI-generated material, and privacy limits for sensitive personal data and student material. Official student pages support claims on Copilot access and student-facing course/discipline guidance. Staff and research pages support advisory, non-overstated claims on examination feedback, AI grading caution, and research AI support. The local AI-guidelines page supports a source-status claim about local guidelines and the university-wide guideline scope. The Faculty of Law source supports one explicitly faculty-scoped assessment restriction claim. A generic staff AI landing page and a research blog post were rejected and not used as evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Evidence is limited to public official Uppsala University pages and PDFs available without login; no login-gated, paywalled, CAPTCHA-protected, or WAF-protected content was accessed.
Research support guidance is staged as guidance only and not as a formal research policy.
The Faculty of Law assessment restriction claim is explicitly scoped to Faculty of Law courses and must not be generalized to all Uppsala University courses.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not marked canonical or human-reviewed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
